title: Dungeon Energy Conduit Simulator
description: A dungeon chamber contains magical energy conduits represented by ShaderBalls placed at key positions. The user must route energy through the dungeon by configuring conduit properties (intensity, frequency, polarity) while obeying energy conservation laws, coupling multiple conduits together, and using undo/redo to refine the network until total energy output matches a target threshold.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements a dungeon energy conduit simulation. Load dungeon_warkarma.glb as the environment and place 5 instances of ShaderBall.glb at predefined positions inside the dungeon acting as energy conduits. Use OrbitControls for camera navigation, starting with a 3/4 overhead view. Add ambient light (0.3 intensity) and 5 point lights (one per conduit, colored by conduit state). Each ShaderBall conduit has three adjustable properties: intensity (0-100 slider), frequency (1-10 integer stepper), and polarity (positive/negative toggle). Display a floating HTML panel when a conduit is clicked via raycasting showing its controls. Implement energy conservation: the total system energy is fixed at 500 units. When one conduit's intensity increases, the others must proportionally decrease to maintain the sum. Implement multi-input coupling: adjacent conduits (within a distance threshold) with matching frequency resonate, doubling their effective output shown as a glow effect using emissive material properties. Mismatched polarity between neighbors causes interference, halving output. Display a HUD showing: total raw energy (must always equal 500), total effective energy (after resonance/interference), and a target effective energy value of 750. The user wins when effective energy is within 5 units of 750. Implement full undo/redo: store snapshots of all conduit states on every change. Bind Ctrl+Z for undo and Ctrl+Shift+Z for redo, also provide on-screen buttons. ShaderBalls should visually reflect their state: emissive color intensity maps to energy level (blue=low, red=high), pulsing animation speed maps to frequency, and polarity toggles between warm and cool color tints. Add particle effects between coupled conduits using a simple Points geometry. The interaction requires at least 10 steps: clicking 5 conduits, adjusting multiple properties, undoing mistakes, and fine-tuning resonance chains to hit the target.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  conduitIntensities: number[5],         // array of intensity values (0-100) for each of the 5 conduits
  conduitFrequencies: number[5],         // array of frequency values (1-10 integers) for each conduit
  conduitPolarities: number[5],         // array of polarity values (+1 or -1) for each conduit
  totalRawEnergy: number,         // sum of all conduit intensities, must equal 500
  totalEffectiveEnergy: number,         // energy after resonance/interference effects applied
  resonancePairCount: number,         // count of adjacent conduit pairs with matching frequency
  interferencePairCount: number,         // count of adjacent conduit pairs with mismatched polarity
  selectedConduitIndex: number,         // index of currently selected conduit (-1 if none)
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance of camera from dungeon center
  lightsInitialized: boolean,         // whether point lights have been created for conduits
  dungeonsLoaded: boolean,         // whether both dungeon and ShaderBall assets are loaded
  activeResonanceCount: number,         // number of conduits currently experiencing resonance boost
}
